prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor for formaldehyde (O = CH₂) at the input geometry; headline value reported is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier “standard”), no density fitting (exact four-center integrals) in PySCF 2.x.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (formaldehyde.xyz, 4 atoms); single-point charge‐partition calculations only – no additional geometry optimisation (notation: Fukui//input-xyz).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model requested).
Charge & multiplicity: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet parent). N + 1 and N – 1 states run as doublets (charge –1/+1, multiplicity 2) per default scheme.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand `fukui`, PySCF backend) with Mulliken charge analysis.
Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles converged for the N, N + 1, and N – 1 states. The engine returned three warnings (verbatim below). Result passes integrity check; integrity.trustworthy = true.
Live log: calculation ran via
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 …
Logging was streamed to the default <subcommand>_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory; path shown at run time (available on request).

Selected results (indices 0-based, order follows input .xyz):

Atom 1  C  f⁺ = 0.7083913    f⁻ = 0.11244422   f⁰ = 0.41041776   Δf = +0.59594709

Atom 3  H  f⁺ = –0.088260145 f⁻ = 0.21005551   f⁰ = 0.060897683  Δf = –0.29831566

Most electrophilic site: Atom 1 (C), f⁺ = 0.7083913 (reported as headline value).
Most nucleophilic site:  Atom 0 (O), f⁻ = 0.46742464.
